purpose: Narrow artifact census of the bootstrap rule ONLY, to resolve the contradiction between the v0.3 frozen design (stationary/10k/seed20260711/expected-L5/+10-session) and the v1.0.3 ValidationOOS prereg + Increment-1 implementation (moving-block/L21/2000/seed42). This census RECORDS; it does not rule. See MR002_Bootstrap_Ruling_Candidate_v1.0.md.
central_question: Did an explicit, owner-approved POST-FREEZE decision change the v0.3 stationary-bootstrap rule? Or is the moving-block spec a source-transcription substitution of the same class as the corrected gate-battery mis-anchoring?

finding: The FROZEN v1.0 lineage (v0.2->v0.3->v1.0-frozen, owner-signed) specifies the STATIONARY bootstrap (10,000 reps, seed 20260711, expected block 5 sessions, +10-session sensitivity, 95% one-sided LB on mean daily net return) and NOWHERE specifies moving-block. The moving-block/L21/2000/seed42 spec exists ONLY in the ValidationOOS review chain, first appearing in PhasePlan v0.2 justified as 'matches the platform convention', propagated forward as 'unchanged', and NEVER supported by an owner decision record. It also DROPPED the registered 10-session block-length sensitivity. This is the same transcription-drift class as the previously-corrected gate-battery mis-anchoring.
boundary: No sealed data read; no performance computed; no code changed by this census.
